coding.name: Coding & Software Engineering
coding.description: Evaluate whether an agent can read, write, debug, and ship code in real repositories.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench
benchmarks.what: Fixes real issues from popular Python repos end-to-end.
benchmarks.pros: Industry standard; tests repo navigation, editing, testing.
benchmarks.cons: Expensive to run; can overfit to public issues.
benchmarks.best_for: Coding agents targeting production engineering workflows.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench Verified
benchmarks.what: 500 curated SWE-bench instances with confirmed problem/solution pairs.
benchmarks.pros: More reliable scores; less noise than full SWE-bench.
benchmarks.cons: Still costly; subset may not match your codebase.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench Lite
benchmarks.what: Smaller, simpler subset designed for faster iteration.
benchmarks.pros: Cheaper; good CI signal for agent improvements.
benchmarks.cons: Easier than full SWE-bench; may not generalize.

benchmarks.name: HumanEval
benchmarks.what: 164 hand-written programming problems with unit tests.
benchmarks.pros: Fast, cheap, reproducible; great for model comparison.
benchmarks.cons: Toy problems; does not test repo-level reasoning.
benchmarks.best_for: Quick model capability checks and academic comparisons.

benchmarks.name: MBPP
benchmarks.what: Mostly Basic Programming Problems — crowd-sourced Python tasks.
benchmarks.pros: Larger and more varied than HumanEval; beginner friendly.
benchmarks.cons: Simpler than real-world coding; limited scope.

reasoning.name: General Reasoning & QA
reasoning.description: Tests multi-step reasoning, tool use, and question answering over text.

benchmarks.name: GAIA
benchmarks.what: 466 tasks across three levels requiring reasoning, browsing, multimodality, and tool use.
benchmarks.pros: Strong signal for general assistant ability; human-aligned difficulty.
benchmarks.cons: Some tasks need live tools; leaderboard costs add up.
benchmarks.best_for: General AI assistants and agentic copilots.

benchmarks.name: MMLU / MMLU-Pro
benchmarks.what: Multiple-choice questions spanning STEM, humanities, and social sciences.
benchmarks.pros: Standardized; easy to run; good for model ranking.

benchmarks.name: LiveBench
benchmarks.what: Questions with answers that change over time to reduce memorization.
benchmarks.pros: Harder to game; tests current knowledge.
benchmarks.cons: Limited coverage; not a full agent test.

web-browser.name: Web & Browser Agents
web-browser.description: Agents that navigate websites, fill forms, and complete tasks in a browser.

benchmarks.name: WebArena
benchmarks.what: 812 reproducible tasks across self-hosted web apps.
benchmarks.pros: Controlled environment; tests realistic web interaction.
benchmarks.cons: Setup heavy; simulated sites differ from live web.
benchmarks.best_for: Browser automation agents and RPA copilots.

benchmarks.name: WebVoyager
benchmarks.what: Tasks on live websites using screenshots and actions.
benchmarks.cons: Noisy; slow; live sites can break evals.
benchmarks.best_for: Agents that must operate on real websites.

benchmarks.name: Mind2Web
benchmarks.what: Diverse web tasks across 137 sites in 31 domains.
benchmarks.pros: Broad domain coverage; good cross-site generalization test.
benchmarks.best_for: Generalist web agents and UI understanding.

desktop-os.name: Desktop & OS Agents
desktop-os.description: Agents that control operating systems, applications, and files.

benchmarks.name: OSWorld
benchmarks.what: 369 tasks across Windows, Ubuntu, and macOS requiring GUI + API reasoning.
benchmarks.cons: Requires VM infrastructure; slow to evaluate.

benchmarks.name: Windows Agent Arena
benchmarks.what: 154 tasks across Windows applications and system settings.
benchmarks.pros: Focused on Windows; enterprise automation scenarios.

multi-agent.name: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
multi-agent.description: Evaluate coordination, delegation, and communication across multiple agents.

benchmarks.name: AgentBench
benchmarks.what: Eight environments spanning reasoning, decision making, coding, and embodied tasks.
benchmarks.pros: Holistic view of agent ability across settings.
benchmarks.cons: Complex setup; not a single narrow score.

benchmarks.name: BenchMAX
benchmarks.what: Tasks designed for agent teams with role specialization.

domain-specific.name: Domain-Specific Agents
domain-specific.description: Benchmarks tuned for math, science, security, and other specialized domains.

benchmarks.name: GSM8K / MATH
benchmarks.what: Grade-school and competition-level math word problems.
benchmarks.pros: Clear right/wrong scoring; cheap to run.
benchmarks.best_for: Agents with a calculator or code-interpreter step.

benchmarks.name: CYBER
benchmarks.what: Capture-the-flag style tasks for offensive security agents.
benchmarks.pros: Practical security scenarios; strong signal for capability.

benchmarks.name: SciWorld
benchmarks.what: Text-based scientific experiments in a simulated lab environment.
